name: Gran Colombia (1821–1831)

colors.symbolism: the blood shed in the struggle for independence

description: Three horizontal stripes — yellow (top, broader), blue, red — with the coat of arms of Gran Colombia in the centre or upper hoist.
history: Gran Colombia was the federation of present-day Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Panama and parts of Peru and Brazil, established under Simón Bolívar after independence from Spain. The yellow-blue-red tricolour, designed by Francisco de Miranda, served as its national flag. After Gran Colombia's dissolution in 1831 the flag was inherited by Colombia, Ecuador and Venezuela in modified forms.

features.role: Coat of arms of Gran Colombia
